title: Becker’s New SB 49 Will Create Incentive For Companies To Build “Solar Canopies” To Generate Enough Electricity To Power Millions Of Homes

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The bill SB 49 aims to incentivize companies to build solar canopies over parking lots to generate electricity.",
    "Solar canopies can generate enough electricity to power millions of homes without requiring additional land.",
    "Pavement constitutes 35%-50% of urban surface area, with 40% of that being parking lots.",
    "California has an estimated 101 square miles of parking lots that could produce 6.5 GW of power if covered by solar canopies.",
    "If California were to cover half of its parking lots with solar canopies, it could generate 13 GW of power.",
    "California's energy agencies estimate that the state needs about 110 GW of new solar power to meet its 100% clean energy target by 2045.",
    "One GW of electricity is equivalent to 1 billion watts, enough to power approximately 250,000 homes."
  ]
}
```
